title: UK Foot-and-Mouth Outbreak -> Livestock Supply Shock
summary: The 2001 UK foot-and-mouth outbreak led to mass culling, export restrictions, and severe disruption to cattle and sheep markets.
mechanism: Disease-control culling reduced marketable livestock supply and export access, forcing repricing across meat and feed channels.
causal_chain: animal disease outbreak triggers emergency control measures
mass culling removes supply and blocks export flows
domestic meat and feed markets reprice on disruption

forecast.note: Bullish domestic meat prices, bearish feed demand.

outcome.realized_note: Livestock supply tightened and export disruption dominated agricultural pricing.
outcome.reversal_note: Normalization came as herds rebuilt and disease controls ended.

title: Asian Avian Flu -> Poultry Cull and Feed Shock
summary: Highly pathogenic avian influenza outbreaks across Asia led to poultry culls, trade restrictions, and shifts in meat demand.
mechanism: Culling tightened poultry supply while fear reduced consumption, redistributing feed demand and pushing substitution into pork and other proteins.
causal_chain: avian flu outbreaks trigger culls and trade curbs
poultry supply falls while consumers temporarily shun chicken
protein substitution and feed demand shifts ripple across agri markets

forecast.note: Bearish poultry demand initially; bullish substitute proteins after supply destruction.

outcome.realized_note: Poultry trade and production were disrupted, with substitution effects into other proteins and feed channels.
outcome.reversal_note: Demand normalized once outbreaks were controlled.

title: H1N1 'Swine Flu' Label -> Pork Demand Shock
summary: The 2009 H1N1 outbreak triggered an initial pork demand scare as consumers and importers reacted to the misleading 'swine flu' label.
mechanism: Even without a true food-safety channel, demand fell because disease framing altered consumer behavior and trade decisions.
causal_chain: H1N1 outbreak gets linked to swine in public messaging
consumer and importer fears cut pork demand
hog prices weaken and substitute protein demand benefits

forecast.note: Bearish lean hogs on demand stigma.

outcome.realized_note: Lean hog markets sold off initially before recovering as the food-safety narrative faded.
outcome.reversal_note: Reversal came once transmission mechanics were better understood.

title: US PEDv Outbreak -> Hog and Pork Supply Tightening
summary: Porcine epidemic diarrhea virus spread rapidly through US hog herds, killing piglets and tightening pork supply into 2014.
mechanism: Piglet mortality reduced future slaughter numbers, creating a delayed but powerful supply squeeze in hog markets.
causal_chain: PEDv spreads across hog operations
piglet mortality reduces future herd throughput
lean hog futures reprice higher on delayed supply shortage

forecast.note: Bullish hogs due to breeding-cycle lag.

outcome.realized_note: US hog prices surged in 2014 as slaughter availability tightened following PEDv losses.

sources.title: PEDv and US Hog Market Effects

title: US Avian Flu Wave -> Egg Price Spike
summary: Highly pathogenic avian influenza waves in US layer flocks tightened egg supply and set up a major price spike into 2015.
mechanism: Loss of layer hens cut egg output sharply, especially for food-processing demand that could not easily substitute away.
causal_chain: avian flu hits US layer flocks
hen losses cut egg production capacity
egg prices jump as processors compete for reduced supply

outcome.realized_note: Egg prices rose sharply as US supply was constrained by flock losses.

sources.title: USDA Avian Influenza Egg Market Analysis

title: 2015 US Avian Flu -> Layer Hen Cull and Egg Inflation
summary: The 2015 US avian influenza outbreak led to one of the largest poultry culls in history and a major egg inflation episode.
mechanism: A sudden supply destruction in commercial layer operations created an acute shortage in shell eggs and egg products.
causal_chain: avian flu spreads through commercial layer farms
mass culls destroy productive capacity
egg product shortages push prices and food inflation higher

forecast.note: Strongly bullish eggs and egg products.

outcome.realized_note: Egg prices spiked and food manufacturers faced tight supply until replacement flocks came online.

title: China African Swine Fever -> Pork Supply Collapse
summary: African swine fever spread across China from 2018 onward, destroying a huge portion of the pig herd and creating a major pork and feed shock.
mechanism: Mass herd liquidation slashed pork supply while also reducing domestic feed demand, creating a two-sided protein/feed market dislocation.
causal_chain: ASF spreads through China's hog sector
culling and mortality shrink the pig herd
pork prices rise while soymeal and feed demand fall
China turns to imports to fill the protein gap

forecast.note: Bullish pork and exporters; bearish feed demand near-term.

outcome.realized_note: Chinese pork prices surged and global exporters benefited, while soybean/feed demand was disrupted by herd losses.
outcome.reversal_note: Normalization came as herds rebuilt and imports adjusted.

sources.title: ASF in China and Global Pork Markets

title: China ASF -> Soymeal Demand Collapse
summary: As ASF reduced China's hog herd, soybean and soymeal demand weakened sharply despite the simultaneous pork shortage.
mechanism: Fewer hogs meant less feed consumption, lowering soymeal demand even while meat inflation surged.
causal_chain: ASF shrinks China's hog herd
feed mills use less soymeal and corn
soy import demand weakens relative to prior expectations

forecast.note: Bearish soymeal demand on herd loss.

outcome.realized_note: Soy demand forecasts were revised lower as the pig herd contraction fed through the feed chain.
outcome.reversal_note: Demand recovered gradually with herd rebuilding.

title: China Pork Import Surge -> Global Protein Repricing
summary: China's post-ASF protein shortage drove heavy pork imports, tightening global export supply and supporting hog prices in key exporting countries.
mechanism: Domestic pork shortages in China pulled global exportable supply toward Chinese buyers, supporting prices elsewhere.
causal_chain: ASF leaves China short of pork supply
Chinese import demand surges into world markets
exporting regions reprice pork higher on tighter available supply

outcome.realized_note: Global pork trade tightened as Chinese buying absorbed exportable volumes.
outcome.reversal_note: Effect faded as Chinese herd rebuilt.

title: European ASF Expansion -> Pork Trade Frictions
summary: ASF cases in European wild boar and pig populations raised trade restrictions and repriced regional pork flows.
mechanism: Trade restrictions on infected zones distorted intra-European pork pricing and export channels.
causal_chain: ASF cases trigger sanitary restrictions
export channels fragment by region
regional pork pricing diverges with trade rerouting

forecast.note: Localized pork dislocations rather than a clean global directional trade.

outcome.realized_note: Trade restrictions caused rerouting and regional spread volatility in pork prices.
outcome.reversal_note: Normalization depended on disease zoning and trade policy changes.

title: 2022 US Avian Flu -> Renewed Egg Inflation
summary: Another major avian flu wave in the US tightened egg supply and fed directly into food inflation in 2022.
mechanism: Commercial flock losses constrained egg output while consumer staples demand stayed firm.
causal_chain: HPAI wave reduces layer flocks
egg supply tightens with stable consumer demand
egg prices jump and food inflation rises

outcome.realized_note: Egg prices surged in 2022 as avian flu reduced supply.

title: Europe Avian Flu Wave -> Poultry Supply Tightening
summary: A severe avian flu season in Europe tightened poultry and egg supply across multiple countries.
mechanism: Disease-related culls reduced poultry output and raised replacement costs across the food chain.
causal_chain: avian flu spreads across commercial operations
culling reduces poultry and egg output
retail and wholesale prices rise on tighter supply

outcome.realized_note: Supply tightened in affected European markets with spillovers into egg and poultry prices.
outcome.reversal_note: Recovery followed restocking and seasonal normalization.

title: Brazil Bird Flu Fears -> Poultry Export Risk Premium
summary: Bird flu detections in Brazil raised the risk of export restrictions for the world's largest chicken exporter.
mechanism: Even limited detections created risk of import bans that could sharply alter world poultry supply flows.
causal_chain: bird flu detections raise trade restriction risk
markets price potential export disruption from Brazil
global poultry balances tighten on supply uncertainty

forecast.note: Bullish global poultry on Brazil export-risk premium.

outcome.realized_note: Export-risk premium briefly entered poultry markets even without a full trade collapse.
outcome.reversal_note: Risk premium faded when full export disruption did not materialize.

title: US Cattle Herd Liquidation -> Beef Supply Tightening
summary: Drought-driven herd liquidation in the US reduced future cattle supply and supported beef prices into 2023-24.
mechanism: Forced liquidation boosts near-term slaughter but shrinks breeding inventory, tightening later supply and lifting deferred cattle prices.
causal_chain: drought and costs force ranchers to liquidate cattle
breeding herd shrinks below replacement pace
future cattle supply tightens and beef prices strengthen

forecast.note: Bullish live cattle on herd-cycle tightening.

outcome.realized_note: US cattle futures and beef prices stayed firm as herd numbers fell to multi-decade lows.
outcome.reversal_note: Longer-term reversal depends on herd rebuilding and pasture conditions.

sources.title: US Cattle Inventory and Herd Liquidation

title: US Dairy Herd H5N1 Discovery -> Milk Supply Risk Premium
summary: H5N1 detections in US dairy cattle introduced a new disease-risk premium into milk and livestock markets.
mechanism: Novel disease transmission into dairy herds raised uncertainty around productivity, culling risk, and interstate movement controls.
causal_chain: novel pathogen detected in dairy herds
markets price potential output and movement disruption
milk and related livestock markets embed risk premium

forecast.note: Bullish dairy risk premium if detections broaden.

outcome.realized_note: The episode introduced a real but still-evolving supply-risk premium rather than a full structural shortage.
outcome.reversal_note: Premium can fade if containment holds.

title: European Bluetongue Resurgence -> Ruminant Supply Concerns
summary: A resurgence of bluetongue disease in parts of Europe renewed concern over cattle and sheep movement restrictions.
mechanism: Movement controls and animal health stress can fragment regional livestock supply and widen local price spreads.
causal_chain: bluetongue cases reappear in livestock regions
movement restrictions disrupt normal marketing channels
regional livestock prices diverge as supply frictions increase

forecast.note: Local bullish supply dislocations more likely than uniform global move.

outcome.realized_note: The main effect was regional supply friction and spread volatility rather than a broad benchmark repricing.
outcome.reversal_note: Spread effects fade as restrictions loosen.
